01 / 03
Digital Systems—2024

Local AI & Technical Workflows

Implementation of local AI experimentation and network configuration to streamline technical optimization workflows.

// Overview

This project explored the deployment of large language models (LLMs) on consumer hardware using tools such as Ollama and LM Studio. The goal was to establish a reproducible local inference environment capable of running 7B–13B parameter models with acceptable latency for engineering documentation tasks.

// Methodology

Hardware benchmarking was performed across multiple quantization levels (Q4, Q5, Q8) to determine the optimal quality/performance trade-off. Port forwarding and local network configuration were tested to expose the inference endpoint to secondary devices on the same network.

// Results

Achieved stable inference at 18–22 tokens/second on a 13B Q4 model without GPU acceleration. Network configuration enabled access from 3 concurrent devices. Average response latency for engineering queries was under 4 seconds.

// Conclusion

Local AI deployment represents a viable path for privacy-sensitive engineering documentation workflows. The configuration established here requires no cloud dependency and can be reproduced on equivalent hardware within 2 hours.
